title: bistorta suffulta subsp. suffulta
intro: Bistorta suffulta subsp. suffulta is a plant species that belongs to the family Polygonaceae, commonly known as bistort or knotweed. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of bistorta suffulta subsp. suffulta include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
